title: From Zero to RAG Agent: Full Beginner's Course (no code)

extracted.hook: By the end of this video, you'll have built your very own Rag AI agent, even if you're starting at the complete beginning, without having to write a single line of code.

accelerate: The pace seems to accelerate when explaining the RAG process and the setup steps.
slow: The pace slows down during detailed explanations of each step in the pipeline and agent creation.

extracted.cta: "If you enjoyed the video or you learned something new, please give it a like. Definitely helps me out a ton."
transitions: So, I'm going to explain it as simply as I can.
And by the end, I think you're going to be really shocked at how much simpler this all is than you initially thought.
Let's not waste any time and get straight into it.

And in order to do that, we need to set up two different things for this vector rag.
So the first part of what we're doing here is the rag pipeline and it's really simple.
So, I said we're going to be setting up a vector database for our AI agent.
And now the second part of the system once our data is actually in that vector database is we have to build an agent that can talk to it.
So now that we've validated that the AI agent can look through the Superbase vector database, we have one issue, which is it has no memory.
The way we're going to set up that memory is we are going to use Postgress because we've already set up our whole soup base thing.
So now if I'm talking to my agent and I say, "Hello, my name is Nate." We're going to send that off.
And so now if I was to come into the AI agent and say, "What's my name?"
Okay, so you've officially just built your first rag pipeline where you got data into a vector database and you built a rag agent that can talk to the data in there as well.
